title: Refactor phase hooks to functions — externalize hardcoded ops
description:
## Parent

sandcastle-dh4 — Phase hooks (closed, the current shell-command implementation)

## Summary

Replace the shell-command based hooks (getHookCommand → issue metadata/env var → sh -c) with plain function hooks. Then externalize all hardcoded side effects (pnpm check, pnpm install, bd close, notifications) into those hooks, removing the inline implementations.

## Lifecycle

prePlan → plan → postPlan
preExecute → implementer → postImplementer → preReviewer → reviewer → postReviewer
preMerge → merge → postMerge

## Hook implementations (config.mts)

| Hook point | Implementation |
|---|---|
| onPreReviewer | pnpm check (was defaultBiomeCheck in execute.mts) |
| onPostMerge | pnpm install (was installDependencies in merge.mts) |
| onPostMerge | bd close + MERGED label (was onMergeComplete callback) |
| onPostReviewer | ntfy notification on execute error |
| onPostMerge | ntfy notification on merge success/failure |

## What gets deleted

- helpers/hooks.mts (shell-command hooks module)
- helpers/hooks.test.mts (shell-command hook tests)
- defaultBiomeCheck() in execute.mts
- installDependencies() in merge.mts
- onMergeComplete callback from merge.mts + main.mts
- notifier parameter from runMergePhase and runExecutionPhase
- Env-var-based hook integration tests in execute.test.mts and merge.test.mts

## What stays separate

- Label callbacks (ExecuteLabelCallbacks) — orchestration plumbing
- onPlanComplete callback — label transition for planned state
- verifyMergedIssues / safety net — stays inline
- onSandboxReady hooks in config.mts — stays as-is
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] PhaseHook, PhaseHookContext, PhaseHooks types defined in helpers/phase-hooks.mts
- [ ] runHooks() utility runs hook arrays with isolated errors (non-fatal)
- [ ] onPrePlan/onPostPlan wired into plan.mts
- [ ] onPreExecute/onPostImplementer/onPreReviewer/onPostReviewer wired into execute.mts
- [ ] onPreMerge/onPostMerge wired into merge.mts
- [ ] pnpm check externalized as onPreReviewer hook (config.mts)
- [ ] pnpm install externalized as onPostMerge hook (config.mts)
- [ ] bd close + MERGED label externalized as onPostMerge hook (config.mts)
- [ ] Notification sends externalized as onPostReviewer/onPostMerge hooks (config.mts)
- [ ] defaultBiomeCheck() removed from execute.mts
- [ ] installDependencies() removed from merge.mts
- [ ] onMergeComplete callback removed from merge.mts + main.mts
- [ ] notifier parameter removed from runMergePhase and runExecutionPhase
- [ ] Shell-command hooks.mts (getHookCommand, runHook, runPhaseHook) deleted
- [ ] hooks.test.mts replaced with runHooks tests
- [ ] Hook integration tests in execute.test.mts and merge.test.mts work with function hooks (not env vars)

title: Wire sandcastle label state machine with crash-revert and resume
description:
## What to build

Wire the existing sandcastle label state machine (helpers/labels.mts) into the orchestrator loop (main.mts) so issues transition through planned -> executing -> reviewing -> executed -> merged as they move through the pipeline. On crash or interrupt, revert labels so the issue reappears in bd ready. On restart, read existing labels to resume where the pipeline left off instead of starting fresh.

The infrastructure already exists:
- helpers/labels.mts has addLabel, addLabelCmd, setMetadata, getMetadata, classifyResumeLabel, shouldSkipPlanner
- phases/plan.mts accepts onPlanComplete
- phases/execute.mts accepts labelCallbacks: ExecuteLabelCallbacks
- phases/merge.mts accepts onMergeComplete

Nothing is wired in main.mts.

## Acceptance criteria

### [1] Wire execute label callbacks in main.mts

- [ ] A createLabelCallbacks() helper (or inline wiring) implements ExecuteLabelCallbacks using labels.mts helpers
- [ ] onImplementStart -> sets sandcastle:executing label on the bead issue
- [ ] onReviewStart -> sets sandcastle:reviewing label
- [ ] onExecuteComplete -> sets sandcastle:executed label
- [ ] onImplementSession / onReviewSession -> persists session IDs via setMetadata (key: implementSession / reviewSession)
- [ ] onValidateSession -> checks session metadata exists via getMetadata
- [ ] The callbacks are passed through main.mts to runExecutionPhase

### [2] Crash error revert

- [ ] When the implementer or reviewer sandbox throws (agent error, timeout, etc.), the issue's sandcastle label is reverted one step
- [ ] If no previous label exists, all sandcastle labels are stripped so the issue reappears in bd ready
- [ ] Revert happens in a try/finally block - never blocks the pipeline for other issues

### [3] Wire plan + merge callbacks

- [ ] After planner selects issues, sandcastle:planned label is set on each (onPlanComplete)
- [ ] After merge completes, sandcastle:merged label is set and the issue is closed via bd close <id> (onMergeComplete)
- [ ] Merge failure does NOT set merged label (issue stays sandcastle:executed)
- [ ] Tests cover all callback paths

### [4] Resume-from-label routing

- [ ] On startup, shouldSkipPlanner() is called: if any issue has labels past planned, skip the planner and route issues directly
- [ ] classifyResumeLabel() determines each issue's routing: execute -> skip planner + re-enter execute phase; merge -> skip planner + execute + go straight to merge phase; skip -> ignore issue
- [ ] If a resume session exists in metadata, pass it as implementSession/reviewSession to runExecutionPhase
- [ ] Stale sessions (metadata present but session file missing) are detected via onValidateSession and fall back to fresh start

## Design notes

The label state machine is:
  planned -> executing -> reviewing -> executed -> merged

Transitions must go through validateTransition() from labels.mts to catch illegal transitions (defensive).

On crash revert: use the lifecycle ordering in labels.mts (LIFECYCLE_ORDER) to determine the previous label. If the issue was reviewing, revert to executing. If it was executing, revert to planned. If it was planned, strip all sandcastle labels (back to raw open).

Pseudo-code for revert:
  const phases = [PLANNED, EXECUTING, REVIEWING, EXECUTED, MERGED];
  const currentIdx = phases.indexOf(currentLabel);
  if (currentIdx > 0) {
    await addLabel(issueId, phases[currentIdx - 1]);  // step back
  } else {
    await removeLabel(issueId, currentLabel);  // strip - back to open
  }

## Blocked by

None - can start immediately

title: notifier: generic notification system with ntfy.sh backend
description:
## What to build

A generic notification system for the sandcastle orchestrator. After merging a commit, notify a summary of what was done. Also notify on errors — phase-level failures and per-issue execution failures.

The notifier module uses a pluggable interface so other backends (Slack, Discord, email) can be added later without changing call sites.

### Design

**Notifier interface** — a contract that all backends implement:

```ts
interface Notifier {
  /** Send a notification. Returns void (fire-and-forget). */
  send(summary: NotificationSummary): Promise<void>;
}

interface NotificationSummary {
  level: 'info' | 'warn' | 'error';
  title: string;        // short headline
  message: string;      // body text (may include markdown)
  tags?: string[];      // e.g. ['merge', 'sandcastle']
}
```

**NtfyNotifier** — first implementation. Reads the ntfy.sh topic URL from `NTFY_TOPIC_URL` env var. Posts JSON via HTTP. Attaches `Tags` and `Priority` headers from the summary.

**Notifier registry** — a simple factory that holds one or more notifier instances. Call sites depend on the registry, not on a specific backend:

```ts
// config.mts — instantiate once
export const notifier = createNotifierRegistry([new NtfyNotifier(process.env.NTFY_TOPIC_URL)]);

// phases call: notifier.send({ level: 'info', title: '...', message: '...' })
```

### Integration points

| Trigger | Level | What goes in the notification |
|---|---|---|
| Branch merged successfully | `info` | Branch name, issue ID, issue title |
| Plan phase fails | `error` | Phase name, error message (truncated) |
| Execute phase fails (overall) | `error` | Phase name, error message |
| Per-issue execution fails | `error` | Issue ID, branch, error message |
| Per-branch merge fails | `warn` | Branch, issue ID, error message |

All notifications are fire-and-forget — a failure to send a notification must never crash or block the orchestrator loop.

### Module layout

`.sandcastle/helpers/notifier.mts` — interface, registry, NtfyNotifier
`.sandcastle/helpers/notifier.test.mts` — unit tests (interface contract, NtfyNotifier HTTP shape, registry dispatch)

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] `Notifier` interface defined with `send(NotificationSummary): Promise\<void\>`
- [ ] `NtfyNotifier` posts to ntfy.sh with correct headers and JSON body
- [ ] Notifier registry supports multiple backends, dispatches to all
- [ ] Merge success notification fires after each branch merge in `runMergePhase`
- [ ] Error notifications fire on plan failure, execute failure (phase + per-issue), and merge failure (per-branch)
- [ ] Notification send failures are caught and logged, never propagated
- [ ] Unit tests cover the interface contract, NtfyNotifier HTTP shape, and registry dispatch
- [ ] `NTFY_TOPIC_URL` env var documented in `.env.example`

## Blocked by

None - can start immediately

title: Single-task mode CLI — pnpm sc:single <issue-id>
description:
## Parent

sandcastle-3zc — Phase-level resume via bd labels so sandcastle picks up where it stopped

## What to build

New CLI entry point. Takes one issue ID, fetches it via `bd show`, runs it through the full execute→review→merge pipeline exactly once, then exits. No poll loop, no planner, no queue — straight-line processing. Reuses `executeOneIssue` and `mergeOneIssue` from the pipeline.

```
pnpm sc:single sandcastle-abc
         │
         ▼
  bd show sandcastle-abc → PlannerIssue
         │
         ▼
  executeOneIssue → reviewer → mergeOneIssue → exit
```

Useful for manual retries, debugging, and CI one-shot runs.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] `pnpm sc:single <id>` runs one issue through execute → review → merge
- [ ] Labels are set during execution (reuses label state machine)
- [ ] Crash is reported and process exits with non-zero code
- [ ] Merge runs after execute completes (sequential, same process)
- [ ] Does NOT poll for new issues — runs once and exits
- [ ] reuses `executeOneIssue` and `mergeOneIssue` from pipeline (no duplication)
- [ ] Script registered in `package.json` as `sc:single`

## Blocked by

- sandcastle-fvi — Startup routing + Effect.ts pipeline assembly

title: Startup routing + Effect.ts pipeline assembly
description:
## Parent

sandcastle-3zc — Phase-level resume via bd labels so sandcastle picks up where it stopped

## What to build

The daemon loop reads all open issues' labels on startup. If any `sandcastle:*` label exists on any issue, the planner is skipped and each issue is routed to the correct phase by its label. Effect.ts Queue wires execution (producer), merge (consumer via `Stream.fromQueue`), and verification (parallel fiber) into one pipeline. Merge starts as soon as the first execution finishes — no batch barrier.

### Routing decision

```
Boot
 ├─ no sandcastle:* labels → runPlanner → all planned → pipeline
 └─ sandcastle:* labels exist → skip planner → route:
      planned/executing/reviewing → execute bucket
      executed → merge bucket
      merged → verification bucket (safety net)
```

### Pipeline architecture

```
Queue.bounded ──┬── Producer fiber (Effect.forEach, concurrency:N)
                │      executeOneIssue → if commits → offer queue
                │
                ├── Consumer fiber (Stream.fromQueue)
                │      take → merge → take → merge (sequential)
                │
                └── Verification fiber (Effect.forEach, concurrency:3)
                       verify merged-but-unclosed tickets
```

All pipe syntax. No `yield*`. No recursion (Stream.fromQueue handles termination naturally when queue shuts down).

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] Cold start (no labels): planner runs, full pipeline executes
- [ ] Resume with mixed labels: planner skipped, each issue routed to correct bucket
- [ ] Producer fiber: per-issue error boundary, one crash never touches others
- [ ] Consumer fiber: `Stream.fromQueue`, sequential merge, per-branch error boundary
- [ ] Verification fiber: `alreadyMerged` bucket in parallel, per-issue error boundary
- [ ] Merge starts before all executions finish (no batch barrier)
- [ ] Queue shutdown propagates cleanly through `Stream.fromQueue` termination
- [ ] All pipe syntax, no `yield*`, no recursion
- [ ] Integration test: daemon loop with mixed-label fixtures covers all routing paths

## Blocked by

- sandcastle-z7c — Execute phase labels
- sandcastle-ete — Merge phase labels + safety net
- sandcastle-dh4 — Phase hooks

title: Phase hooks — pre/post commands for execute and merge
description:
## Parent

sandcastle-3zc — Phase-level resume via bd labels so sandcastle picks up where it stopped

## What to build

Configurable shell commands that run before and after each phase. Hooks use `zx` for execution. Each hook has its own error boundary — a failed pre-hook logs a warning and the phase still runs. A failed post-hook logs but doesn't revert the phase result.

```
preExecuteHook ──► executeOneIssue ──► postExecuteHook
preMergeHook    ──► mergeOneIssue   ──► postMergeHook
```

Hook commands are defined in issue metadata or global config:
- `sandcastle.pre_execute_hook` / `sandcastle.post_execute_hook`
- `sandcastle.pre_merge_hook` / `sandcastle.post_merge_hook`

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] `preExecuteHook`, `postExecuteHook` configurable per issue (via metadata) or via global config
- [ ] `preMergeHook`, `postMergeHook` configurable
- [ ] Pre-hook failure: logs warning, phase still executes
- [ ] Post-hook failure: logs warning, phase result preserved
- [ ] Hook commands run via `zx` (built on sandcastle-73h)
- [ ] Hooks isolated: crash in hook-A doesn't affect hook-B or the phase itself
- [ ] Hooks work with both execute phase (sandcastle-z7c) and merge phase (sandcastle-ete)
- [ ] Integration test: hook failure + phase success, hook success + phase crash (both directions)

## Blocked by

- sandcastle-73h — zx shell refactor
- sandcastle-z7c — Execute phase labels
- sandcastle-ete — Merge phase labels + safety net

title: Merge phase sets labels + git safety net + bd ticket close
description:
## Parent

sandcastle-3zc — Phase-level resume via bd labels so sandcastle picks up where it stopped

## What to build

The merge phase (`phases/merge.mts`) sets `sandcastle:merged` on successful merge. Per-branch error boundary ensures one failed merge doesn't block remaining branches in the queue.

On resume, issues already labeled `sandcastle:merged` get a safety-net double-check:
- `git branch --merged` confirms the branch is actually merged
- `bd show --json` confirms the ticket is closed
- If branch merged but ticket open → `bd close` runs
- If label says merged but branch NOT merged → label reverted to `executed` for re-merge

Uses `zx` for all git/pnpm shell commands (built on sandcastle-73h).

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] Successful merge sets `sandcastle:merged` label
- [ ] Merge crash: label stays `sandcastle:executed`
- [ ] Per-branch error boundary: branch-B still merges after branch-A crashes
- [ ] Safety net (resume): label says `merged`, branch IS merged, ticket IS open → `bd close` runs
- [ ] Safety net (resume): label says `merged`, branch NOT merged → label reverted to `executed` for re-merge
- [ ] All shell commands use `zx` (not `child_process`)
- [ ] Integration test: merge crash isolation + safety net stale ticket close

## Blocked by

- sandcastle-73h — zx shell refactor
- sandcastle-8dr — Label state machine

title: Execute phase sets labels + isolates per-issue failures
description:
## Parent

sandcastle-3zc — Phase-level resume via bd labels so sandcastle picks up where it stopped

## What to build

The execute phase (`phases/execute.mts`) sets labels at each transition. Each issue's sandbox lifecycle is isolated so a crash in one issue never affects another issue's label state.

### Label transitions during execute

```
Issue enters       executes        runs impl        runs reviewer       exits
  execute    ──►  label ──► [sandbox] ──► reviewing label ──► [sandbox] ──► executed label
                   │                                                        │
              crash? → label stays "executing"                     crash? → label stays "reviewing"
```

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] Issue entering execute gets labeled `sandcastle:executing`
- [ ] Before reviewer, label transitions to `sandcastle:reviewing`
- [ ] After reviewer completes, label transitions to `sandcastle:executed`
- [ ] Implementer crash: label stays `sandcastle:executing`, other parallel issues unaffected and reach `executed`
- [ ] Reviewer crash: label stays `sandcastle:reviewing`, other parallel issues unaffected and reach `executed`
- [ ] Zero-commit issue (implementer produces nothing): label goes straight to `sandcastle:executed`, no reviewer label set
- [ ] Integration test: 3 issues in parallel, crash one mid-implementer, assert other 2 land at `executed`

## Blocked by

- sandcastle-8dr — Label state machine

title: sandcastle: label state machine + cleanup script
description:
## Parent

sandcastle-3zc — Phase-level resume via bd labels so sandcastle picks up where it stopped

## What to build

A label module that wraps `bd update --label` and `bd metadata set/get` as isolated functions. Implements the label state machine validator that rejects backward transitions. A `pnpm sandcastle:cleanup` script strips all `sandcastle:*` labels from every open issue.

### Label state machine

```
sandcastle:planned → sandcastle:executing → sandcastle:reviewing → sandcastle:executed → sandcastle:merged
```

### API

- `addLabel(issueId, label)` — add a label via bd CLI
- `removeLabel(issueId, label)` — remove a label
- `hasLabel(issueId, label)` — check if label exists on issue
- `getResumePhase(issue)` — returns the current sandcastle label or `null` for cold start
- `validateTransition(from, to)` — throws if transition is invalid (e.g. `executed → executing`)
- `setMetadata(issueId, key, value)` / `getMetadata(issueId, key)` — label-scoped key-value pairs
- `cleanupAllSandcastleLabels()` — strips all `sandcastle:*` labels from every open issue

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] `addLabel(issueId, "sandcastle:planned")` adds the label via bd CLI
- [ ] `removeLabel(issueId, label)` removes it
- [ ] `hasLabel(issueId, label)` returns boolean
- [ ] `getResumePhase` returns the current label from the state machine (or null)
- [ ] Invalid transitions (`executed → executing`) throw
- [ ] `pnpm sandcastle:cleanup` strips all `sandcastle:*` from every open issue
- [ ] Metadata get/set works for label-scoped key-value pairs
- [ ] Unit tests cover all label CRUD operations and transition validation

## Blocked by

None — can start immediately

title: Replace child_process.exec with zx for shell commands
description:
## Parent

sandcastle-3zc — Phase-level resume via bd labels so sandcastle picks up where it stopped

## What to build

Currently `phases/merge.mts` uses `promisify(exec)` from `child_process` for shell execution. The `helpers/issues.mts` already uses `zx`'s `$`. Unify all shell commands on `zx` for consistent quoting, color output, and pipe support.

The `ShellExec` type signature stays the same so existing tests pass without modification. Only the default implementation changes.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] `defaultShell` uses `zx` `$` instead of `promisify(exec)`
- [ ] `ShellExec` type unchanged (tests don't break)
- [ ] `zx` quiet mode by default (`$.verbose = false`)
- [ ] Existing merge phase tests pass
- [ ] No other `child_process` usage remains in `.sandcastle/src/`

## Blocked by

None — can start immediately

title: Move source code to .sandcastle/src/
description:
## Parent

sandcastle-3zc — Phase-level resume via bd labels so sandcastle picks up where it stopped

## What to build

Move all `.mts` source files from `.sandcastle/` to `.sandcastle/src/`, preserving the subdirectory structure. Move prompt `.md` files to `.sandcastle/prompts/`. Update all import paths. Update `tsconfig.json` and `vitest.config.ts`. Zero behavior change — all existing tests must pass identically.

```
Before:                              After:
.sandcastle/                         .sandcastle/
├── config.mts                       ├── src/
├── types.mts                        │   ├── config.mts
├── helpers/                         │   ├── types.mts
│   ├── concurrency.mts              │   ├── helpers/
│   └── issues.mts                   │   │   ├── concurrency.mts
├── phases/                          │   │   └── issues.mts
│   ├── execute.mts                  │   ├── phases/
│   ├── merge.mts                    │   │   ├── execute.mts
│   └── plan.mts                     │   │   ├── merge.mts
├── main.mts                         │   │   └── plan.mts
├── implement-prompt.md              │   └── main.mts
├── merge-prompt.md                  ├── prompts/
├── plan-prompt.md                   │   ├── implement.md
├── review-prompt.md                 │   ├── merge.md
├── *.test.mts                       │   ├── plan.md
└── ...                              │   └── review.md
                                     ├── *.test.mts
                                     └── ...
```

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] All `.mts` source files moved to `.sandcastle/src/`, preserving subdirectory structure
- [ ] All import paths updated to new locations
- [ ] Prompt `.md` files moved to `.sandcastle/prompts/`
- [ ] Prompt file path references updated in code
- [ ] `tsconfig.json` `include` updated
- [ ] `vitest.config.ts` updated if needed
- [ ] `pnpm test` — all 8 existing test suites pass
- [ ] `pnpm typecheck` — no errors
- [ ] `pnpm sc` — daemon starts without import errors

## Blocked by

None — can start immediately
close_reason: Completed: moved source to .sandcastle/src/ and prompts to .sandcastle/prompts/

title: [feature] Agent session resume — continue implementer/reviewer from last session
description:
## Parent

sandcastle-3zc — Phase-level resume via bd labels

## What to build

When sandcastle resumes an issue that was mid-execution (labels `sandcastle:executing` or `sandcastle:reviewing`), reuse the agent's previous session via `resumeSession` instead of starting the implementer or reviewer from scratch. Saves API costs and avoids redoing work.

### Session storage

During execute, when an agent (implementer or reviewer) produces a session, store the session ID as metadata on the bead issue:

- `sandcastle.implement_session=<sessionId>` — set after implementer run produces a session
- `sandcastle.review_session=<sessionId>` — set after reviewer run produces a session

### Resume logic

When `executeOneIssue` encounters an issue labeled `sandcastle:executing` with `sandcastle.implement_session` metadata:

1. Create sandbox on the issue's branch (same as fresh run)
2. Pass `resumeSession` to `sandbox.run()` for the implementer
3. If session file is missing (stale metadata from a failed session capture), fall back to fresh implementer start
4. After implementer completes, continue to reviewer (resume reviewer session if available, otherwise fresh)

When encountering `sandcastle:reviewing` with `sandcastle.review_session` metadata:

1. Skip implementer (already done)
2. Pass `resumeSession` to `sandbox.run()` for the reviewer
3. Same stale-metadata fallback

### Loop continuity

The execute→review loop continues where the crash left it. If crash was mid-implementer, resume implementer then run reviewer fresh. If crash was mid-reviewer, resume reviewer directly.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] Implementer session ID stored as `sandcastle.implement_session` metadata on issue after run
- [ ] Reviewer session ID stored as `sandcastle.review_session` metadata on issue after run
- [ ] Issue labeled `sandcastle:executing` with valid session: implementer resumes from last session
- [ ] Issue labeled `sandcastle:reviewing` with valid session: reviewer resumes from last session (implementer skipped)
- [ ] Stale session (file missing): falls back to fresh agent start, logs warning
- [ ] Issue labeled `sandcastle:planned` (no session metadata): starts implementer fresh (backward compatible)
- [ ] Reviewer always runs after implementer completes, whether implementer was fresh or resumed

title: [feature] Phase-level resume via bd labels so sandcastle picks up where it stopped
description:
## Parent

sandcastle-tee — Add error boundary to main loop so one task failure doesn't crash the process

## What to build

Give sandcastle the ability to resume from where it stopped after a crash or manual stop. State is persisted as labels and metadata on bead issues — no separate state file.

### Label state machine (per issue)

```
sandcastle:planned → sandcastle:executing → sandcastle:reviewing → sandcastle:executed → sandcastle:merged
```

Each label transition also stores metadata:
- `sandcastle:executing` stores `sandcastle.implement_session=<sessionId>`
- `sandcastle:reviewing` stores `sandcastle.review_session=<sessionId>`

### Resume decision on startup

| Labels on any open issue | Behavior |
|---|---|
| Only `sandcastle:planned` | Run planner fresh, then execute + merge |
| Any `executing`, `reviewing`, `executed`, `merged` | Bypass planner, route each issue to correct phase |

### Per-issue routing on resume

| Label | Action |
|---|---|
| `sandcastle:planned` | Start execute fresh (implement → review) |
| `sandcastle:executing` | Resume implementer on correct branch |
| `sandcastle:reviewing` | Resume reviewer on correct branch |
| `sandcastle:executed` | Skip execute, proceed to merge |
| `sandcastle:merged` | Skip entirely |

### Merge phase safety net

Labels are authoritative. `git branch --merged` is a secondary check:
- Label says `merged` but branch not merged → re-merge
- Branch merged but label missing → labels get updated

### Cleanup

`pnpm sandcastle:cleanup` script strips all `sandcastle:*` labels. No automatic cleanup.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] Planner labels each planned issue `sandcastle:planned`
- [ ] Execute phase adds `sandcastle:executing` label when implementer starts
- [ ] Execute phase transitions to `sandcastle:reviewing` when reviewer starts
- [ ] Execute phase transitions to `sandcastle:executed` when implement + review complete
- [ ] Merge phase sets `sandcastle:merged` on successful merge
- [ ] On resume with only `planned` labels: planner runs fresh
- [ ] On resume with execute/review/merge labels: planner skipped, issues routed to correct phase
- [ ] `git branch --merged` fallback catches merged-but-unlabeled branches in merge phase
- [ ] `pnpm sandcastle:cleanup` removes all `sandcastle:*` labels
- [ ] Cold start (no sandcastle labels) runs the full plan→execute→merge loop normally

title: Vitest polish — coverage, cleanup, CI-ready
description:
## Parent
sandcastle-7ks (closed, superseded)

## What to build
Once all test files are migrated and passing under vitest: add coverage reporting config (c8 or v8 provider), verify exit codes work for CI, remove legacy `tsx` test entries from package.json scripts, and confirm the full suite passes with a single `pnpm test` (vitest run).

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] `vitest run --coverage` produces a coverage report

- sandcastle-jkl

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] concurrency.test.mts uses vitest describe/it/expect and all assertions pass

title: Migrate core tests to vitest
description:
## Parent
sandcastle-7ks

## What to build
Convert `.sandcastle/types.test.mts` and `.sandcastle/config.test.mts` from raw node:assert + tsx runner to vitest (describe/it/expect). All type and config tests pass under vitest runner.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] types.test.mts uses vitest describe/it/expect and all assertions pass

title: [fix] Add error boundary to main loop so one task failure doesn't crash the process
description:
## Root cause

The main orchestration loop in `.sandcastle/main.mts` has no error boundary. When any phase throws, the uncaught rejection propagates to Node.js ESM runner (`run_main:107 triggerUncaughtException`) and kills the entire process.

### Failure points (all uncaught)

| Phase | Call site | What happens |
|-------|-----------|--------------|
| Phase 1 (Plan) | `sandcastle.run({...})` — planner agent fails or produces no `<plan>` tag | Process crashes |
| Phase 2 (Execute) | `executeOneIssue` → `sandbox.run()` — individual promise rejections ARE caught by `runWithConcurrencyLimit`, but process-level unhandled rejections from Effect defects (`orDie`, uncaught `Effect.runPromise`) bypass it | Process crashes |
| Phase 3 (Merge) | `runMergePhase` → `sandcastle.run()` — merge agent failure in the sequential loop | Process crashes |

### Example crash (from merge phase)

```
issueId: "sandcastle-72m"
[merger] Started on branch sandcastle/merger/20260514-093832
Worktree removed (no uncommitted changes)
node:internal/modules/run_main:107
    triggerUncaughtException(
```

## What to build

Add defense-in-depth error isolation:

1. **Phase-level try/catch** — wrap each phase in the main loop so one phase failure logs and continues (or exits the iteration cleanly)
2. **Per-branch merge isolation** — if one merge fails in `runMergePhase`, log and skip that branch, continue merging remaining branches
3. **Process-level `unhandledRejection` handler** — safety net that logs the rejection instead of crashing, preventing stray Effect defects from killing the process

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] Main loop continues when Phase 1 (plan) throws — logs error, breaks out of loop (planner failing means no work to do)
- [ ] Main loop continues when individual Phase 2 tasks fail — other parallel tasks unaffected, failed tasks logged with issue ID
- [ ] Main loop continues when Phase 3 (merge) of one branch fails — remaining branches still merged
- [ ] Process-level `unhandledRejection` handler prevents crash from stray Effect defects
- [ ] All failures logged at `error` level with enough context to identify which issue/branch failed
acceptance_criteria:
- [x] Main loop continues when Phase 1 (plan) throws — logs error, breaks out of loop (planner failing means no work to do)
- [x] Main loop continues when individual Phase 2 tasks fail — other parallel tasks unaffected, failed tasks logged with issue ID  
- [x] Main loop continues when Phase 3 (merge) of one branch fails — remaining branches still merged
- [x] Process-level unhandledRejection handler prevents crash from stray Effect defects
- [x] All failures logged at error level with enough context to identify which issue/branch failed
notes: RALPH: Added error boundary to main loop. Changes: Phase 1 (plan) failure logged, loop exits. Phase 2 (execute) failure logged, continues loop. Phase 3 (merge) two-layer defense (per-branch try/catch + outer try/catch). unhandledRejection handler added. New tests in merge.test.mts for per-branch isolation. All 8 test suites pass, typecheck clean.

title: Make merge phase sequential — merge one branch at a time
description:
## Problem Statement

Currently, the merger phase passes all completed branches to a single `sandcastle.run()` call. One agent receives the full list of branches and merges them all at once. If a merge fails or introduces conflicts, the agent must reason about multiple branches simultaneously, which increases complexity and makes failure isolation harder.

## Proposed Solution

Process branches sequentially instead of in a batch. After the execute phase completes, iterate over each completed branch one at a time and merge it into the target branch before proceeding to the next.

## Open Questions

- Should each branch get its own `sandcastle.run()` call, or should one agent run handle them sequentially?
- If a sequential merge fails (conflicts), should the process abort, skip that branch, or escalate to human review?
- Should there be a review step between each merge?
- How does this interact with the `merge-to-head` branch strategy for the merger sandbox?

## Out of Scope

- Changing how the execute phase works
- Changing the review logic inside the execute phase

title: Refactor .sandcastle/main.mts for readability and daemonize the orchestration loop
description:
## Problem Statement

.sandcastle/main.mts has grown into a single 260-line file that mixes configuration, helper functions, type schemas, and three distinct orchestration phases (plan, execute, merge) inside a top-level `for` loop. The file is hard to read, hard to navigate, and hard to test. There is no clear entry point (code runs on import), no separation of concerns, and the `runWithConcurrencyLimit` helper uses a non-idiomatic `Promise.race` cleanup pattern that is difficult to follow.

Additionally, the current loop is capped at `MAX_ITERATIONS = 10`, which means the service must be restarted manually to continue processing. The user wants this to run as a long-lived systemd service that polls forever.

## Solution

Refactor main.mts into a small, focused module tree:

- `.sandcastle/config.mts` — environment, constants, pino logger (with pretty-print for TTY)
- `.sandcastle/types.mts` — shared Zod schemas and TypeScript types
- `.sandcastle/helpers/concurrency.mts` — cleaned-up `runWithConcurrencyLimit`
- `.sandcastle/helpers/issues.mts` — `getOpenIssues`, `waitUntilThereAreOpenIssues`
- `.sandcastle/phases/plan.mts` — Phase 1: planner sandbox run
- `.sandcastle/phases/execute.mts` — Phase 2: per-issue implement + review
- `.sandcastle/phases/merge.mts` — Phase 3: merge completed branches
- `.sandcastle/main.mts` — thin orchestrator: `main()` function, `while (true)` loop, SIGTERM handling, heartbeat logging

Replace `MAX_ITERATIONS` with an infinite `while (true)` loop. Add a `SIGTERM` handler that allows the current iteration to finish gracefully but forces exit after 10 minutes. Log a heartbeat at the start of each iteration. Use pino for structured logging; pretty-print when stdout is a TTY, JSON otherwise (systemd journal). Update the systemd service file and README to document the new daemon behavior.

## Commits

Each commit leaves the codebase in a working state.

**Commit 1: Install pino and pino-pretty, extract config module**
- `pnpm add pino` and `pnpm add -D pino-pretty`
- Create `.sandcastle/config.mts` containing:
  - `resolveHostPath()`
  - `sandboxMounts` construction and validation
  - `readOpencodeApiKey()`
  - `sandboxProvider` initialization
  - Pino logger setup (pretty transport when `process.stdout.isTTY` is true, raw JSON otherwise)
  - Constants: `MAX_PARALLEL_TASKS`, `POLL_INTERVAL_MS`, `GRACEFUL_SHUTDOWN_MS`
- Update `.sandcastle/main.mts` to import config instead of defining inline

**Commit 2: Extract types and schemas to types.mts**
- Create `.sandcastle/types.mts` with `BeadsIssueSchema`, `PlannerOutputSchema`, and exported `BeadsIssue` type
- Update `.sandcastle/main.mts` to import schemas/types from `types.mts`

**Commit 3: Extract issue helpers to helpers/issues.mts**
- Create `.sandcastle/helpers/issues.mts` with `getOpenIssues()` and `waitUntilThereAreOpenIssues()`
- Replace `console.log` calls inside helpers with `logger.info` / `logger.error` from config
- Update `.sandcastle/main.mts` to import from `helpers/issues.mts`

**Commit 4: Extract concurrency helper with simplified implementation**
- Create `.sandcastle/helpers/concurrency.mts` with `runWithConcurrencyLimit()`
- Rewrite the internals to use a `Set<Promise<void>>` and `.finally(() => executing.delete(p))` instead of the nested `Promise.race` cleanup loop
- Update `.sandcastle/main.mts` to import from `helpers/concurrency.mts`

**Commit 5: Extract plan phase to phases/plan.mts**
- Create `.sandcastle/phases/plan.mts` exporting `runPlanner()`
- The function performs the sandbox run, extracts the `<plan>` XML block, parses JSON, validates with `PlannerOutputSchema`, and returns the issue list
- Accepts logger and sandbox provider as arguments
- Update `.sandcastle/main.mts` to call `runPlanner()` instead of inline code

**Commit 6: Extract execute phase to phases/execute.mts**
- Create `.sandcastle/phases/execute.mts` exporting `runExecutionPhase()`
- The function takes the issue list, creates sandboxes per issue, runs implementer then reviewer, collects completed branches, and returns the results
- Uses `runWithConcurrencyLimit` internally
- Accepts logger, sandbox provider, hooks, and `copyToWorktree` as arguments
- Update `.sandcastle/main.mts` to call `runExecutionPhase()` instead of inline code

**Commit 7: Extract merge phase to phases/merge.mts**
- Create `.sandcastle/phases/merge.mts` exporting `runMergePhase()`
- The function takes completed issues and branches, runs the merger sandbox, and returns void
- Accepts logger and sandbox provider as arguments
- Update `.sandcastle/main.mts` to call `runMergePhase()` instead of inline code

**Commit 8: Refactor main.mts entry point for daemon behavior**
- Wrap the orchestration loop in an async `main()` function
- Replace `for (let iteration = 1; iteration <= MAX_ITERATIONS; iteration++)` with `while (true)` and a manual `iteration` counter
- Add `SIGTERM` handler:
  - Set a `shouldShutdown` flag to true
  - Start a 10-minute timer; if the loop hasn't exited by then, call `process.exit(1)`
- At the top of each loop iteration, log a heartbeat via pino (include iteration count and process uptime)
- After Phase 3, check `shouldShutdown`; if true, log shutdown message and break the loop
- Add `main().catch(err => { logger.fatal(err); process.exit(1); })` at module level
- Remove all now-extracted inline code from main.mts

**Commit 9: Install vitest and add unit tests**
- `pnpm add -D vitest`
- Create `.sandcastle/helpers/concurrency.test.mts`:
  - Test that `runWithConcurrencyLimit` respects the concurrency limit
  - Test that all promises are settled even when some reject
  - Test that results are in the correct order
- Create `.sandcastle/helpers/issues.test.mts`:
  - Test `getOpenIssues` success path (mock `zx` output)
  - Test `getOpenIssues` error path returns empty array
- Update `package.json` test script to `vitest run`
- Add `vitest.config.ts` with `NodeNext` module resolution if needed

**Commit 10: Update systemd service and documentation**
- Update `.sandcastle/systemd/sandcastle.service`:
  - Add `TimeoutStopSec=630` (10 minutes + 30 second buffer for graceful shutdown)
  - Remove stale comments about `MAX_ITERATIONS`
- Update `.sandcastle/systemd/README.md`:
  - Document the infinite poll loop
  - Document SIGTERM / graceful shutdown behavior
  - Document heartbeat logs and how to read them with `journalctl`
  - Document pino JSON log format

## Decision Document

- **Module boundaries:** Config, types, helpers, and phases each get their own file. No cross-import cycles: main imports everything; phases import helpers and types; helpers import types and config.
- **Logger interface:** All modules accept a pino `Logger` instance as an argument rather than importing it directly. This keeps modules testable (pass a silent test logger) and avoids hidden global state.
- **Entry point shape:** `main.mts` exports no named exports; it calls `main()` at module load and handles the promise. This is acceptable for a CLI entry point. If tests ever need to import the loop logic, `main()` can be exported later.
- **SIGTERM behavior:** The process handles SIGTERM by setting a boolean flag. The current iteration completes (plan → execute → merge), then the loop checks the flag and exits cleanly. A 10-minute fallback timer ensures the process dies even if an agent hangs.
- **systemd integration:** `Restart=on-failure` is kept. `TimeoutStopSec=630` gives systemd awareness of the graceful shutdown window. The process itself also enforces the 10-minute limit to avoid relying solely on systemd.
- **Pretty print:** Detected via `process.stdout.isTTY`. When running under systemd, stdout is not a TTY, so logs are raw JSON suitable for `journalctl -o json`.
- **Infinite loop:** `while (true)` replaces `MAX_ITERATIONS`. The only exit conditions are SIGTERM, an unhandled exception (which crashes the process and triggers systemd restart), or a catastrophic error inside the loop that isn't caught.
- **Error handling in the loop:** Errors thrown during plan/execute/merge are logged at `fatal` level and rethrown, causing the process to crash. systemd restarts the service. This matches the user's preference for "crash and let systemd restart it."

## Testing Decisions

- **Test external behavior, not implementation details:** Tests for `runWithConcurrencyLimit` verify that the limit is respected and that the returned array preserves input order. They do not inspect the internal `Set` or promise mechanics.
- **Mock external dependencies:** `getOpenIssues` tests mock the `zx` shell invocation and `bd` JSON output. No tests hit the real `bd` database or network.
- **Test modules:**
  - `.sandcastle/helpers/concurrency.mts`
  - `.sandcastle/helpers/issues.mts`
- **Prior art:** There are currently no tests in the codebase. This commit establishes the testing convention using vitest.
- **Out of scope for tests:** Phases (plan, execute, merge) are not unit-tested in this refactor because they depend on the `sandcastle.run()` SDK and Docker sandboxes, which are impractical to mock meaningfully without significant extra work. They can be covered by integration tests in future work.

## Out of Scope

- Extracting the prompt files (`.sandcastle/*-prompt.md`) into a different structure
- Changing the sandcastle agent model (`opencode-go/kimi-k2.6`)
- Changing the review logic (e.g., running reviewer only when commits exist)
- Adding integration or end-to-end tests for the full orchestration loop
- Docker image changes or sandbox provider changes
- Changing the `copyToWorktree` or `hooks` definitions
- Replacing `zx` with a different shell library
- Metrics, alerting, or health-check endpoints beyond the heartbeat log line

## Further Notes

- The existing `.sandcastle/logs/` directory and its contents are untouched.
- The `Containerfile`, `.env.example`, `.gitignore`, and prompt files remain unchanged.
- After this refactor, `.sandcastle/main.mts` should be under 80 lines of orchestration code.
